title: NOC 92023 – Supervisors, other mechanical and metal products manufacturing | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 92023 are the technical leaders of Canada’s diverse "General Manufacturing" sector. This TEER 2 occupation covers the production of a wide array of mechanical and metal goods, including heavy machinery, industrial tools, metal containers, and structural metal products. In 2026, as Canada ramps up its industrial capacity for the green energy transition and infrastructure renewal, these supervisors are critical for managing high-precision fabrication and assembly lines. For immigration, this role is a top-tier candidate for the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and Express Entry Category-Based Selection.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Level2Major Group92 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsSub-Major Group920 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsMinor Group9202 – Supervisors, assembly and fabricationNOC Code92023

duties: Supervision: Coordinate and schedule the activities of workers who assemble, fabricate, and inspect mechanical and metal products.
Operational Strategy: Establish methods to meet strict work schedules and coordinate activities with engineering, procurement, and quality assurance.
Technical Troubleshooting: Resolve complex work problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and product tolerance levels.
Resource Management: Requisition materials and supplies; manage equipment maintenance and high-tech tool calibration schedules.
Staff Development: Train workers in job duties, advanced safety procedures (including robotic safety), and company policies.
Quality Assurance: Ensure strict adherence to safety rules, environmental regulations, and ISO 9001/AS9100 quality standards.
Reporting: Prepare detailed production logs, safety audit reports, and material utilization summaries for plant management.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Work Experience: Several years of experience in the specific mechanical or metal processing occupation supervised are mandatory (e.g., as a machinist, welder, or assembler).
Education: A college diploma or vocational training in mechanical engineering technology, machinist trade, or industrial management is often required.
Technical Skills: Proficiency in CAD/CAM software, Lean Manufacturing (Six Sigma), and ERP systems.
Strong leadership, mechanical aptitude, and the ability to interpret complex technical drawings and blueprints are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, supervisors in specialized fields like Aerospace Component Fabrication or Nuclear Power Hardware often earn a 20-25% premium above the median industrial wage.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the mechanical products sector will see a 11% growth in supervisory positions by 2033. The primary driver is "Industrial Re-shoring," with Canadian firms increasing local production of essential mechanical goods to avoid global supply chain disruptions. Replacement demand due to the retirement of senior plant foremen remains high across the manufacturing belt of Southern Ontario.
labour_market_demand: The demand for mechanical and metal product supervisors is Very Good, particularly in provinces with strong industrial bases. In 2026, Ontario, Alberta, and Quebec are reporting a high need for supervisors to manage the fabrication of components for wind turbines, electric vehicle charging stations, and modular construction units. As the industry moves toward "Agile Manufacturing," there is a specialized demand for supervisors who can oversee the integration of Collaborative Robots (Cobots) and advanced CNC machining centers.
Industrial Diversification: Work across multiple sub-sectors, from aerospace components and heavy equipment to high-tech medical hardware.
Technological Advancement: Manage "Industry 4.0" shops featuring AI-driven predictive maintenance and additive manufacturing (3D Metal Printing).
Stable Economic Foundation: Mechanical and metal products manufacturing is a multi-billion dollar pillar of the Canadian economy with robust export ties to the US.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 2 role is highly prioritized in Express Entry draws for Trades and various provincial In-Demand Skills streams.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Highly targeted for candidates with experience in "Trade Occupations."
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): A direct route for those with a job offer from an Ontario-based mechanical or metal manufacturer.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Priority for supervisors supporting the province’s massive industrial fabrication and energy-service sectors.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority for supervisors in the high-growth aerospace and marine manufacturing clusters on the West Coast.
